title: Release process — mixeff
text: one canonical acceptance command, run clean checkout: builds tarball, runs R CMD check ---cran tarball (never source directory), lints, runs fast test suite + error-UX battery + schema/manifest tests installed release build, runs offline-install gate. writes release-gate-report.txt — file acceptance artifact attach R-universe / CRAN submission. Exit status non-zero gate fails. Use --fast skip two slowest gates (R CMD check, offline install) iteration.

title: Benchmarking mixeff
text: benchmark sweeps five mixed-model designs — varying rows, varying subject levels, adding random slopes, crossing subject item, combining crossed grouping random slope — across log-spaced grid scale values. cell, mixeff::lmm() lme4::lmer() fit model three times. summary table records median elapsed seconds speedup lme4 per cell. first figure plots median fit time design scale, log-log axes, one panel per scenario. mixeff runs lme4 everywhere grid; gap widens design complexity grows.  second figure folds data one panel: speedup mixeff lme4 cell. dashed reference line 1× mean two engines equally fast.  Read speedup figure caveats deserves. Three replications per cell give stable median wide envelope; absolute timings sub-15 ms throughout, extra millisecond system noise moves ratio visibly. qualitative pattern robust: mixeff faster everywhere, speedup grows design’s complexity — exactly regime bootstrap workflows spend time.
code: benchmark_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "lme4-scaling-summary.csv",   package = "mixeff" ) benchmark <- read.csv(benchmark_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  benchmark$scenario_label <- factor(   benchmark$scenario,   levels = c("rows", "groups", "slopes", "crossed", "crossed_slope"),   labels = c(     "vary rows (1 | subject)",     "vary subject levels (1 | subject)",     "add random slope (1 + x | subject)",     "crossed (1 | subject) + (1 | item)",     "crossed + slope (1 + x | subject) + (1 | item)"   ) )  head(benchmark[, c("scenario", "scale_value", "engine",                    "median_sec", "speedup_vs_lme4")]) #>   scenario scale_value engine median_sec speedup_vs_lme4 #> 1  crossed          10   lme4      0.008        1.000000 #> 2  crossed          10 mixeff      0.002        4.000000 #> 3  crossed          15   lme4      0.010        1.000000 #> 4  crossed          15 mixeff      0.003        3.333333 #> 5  crossed          20   lme4      0.011        1.000000 #> 6  crossed          20 mixeff      0.004        2.750000 library(ggplot2)  ggplot(benchmark,        aes(scale_value, median_sec * 1000, colour = engine,            group = engine)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~ scenario_label, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +   scale_x_log10() +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(lme4 = "#888888", mixeff = "#3366cc")) +   labs(     x = "design scale (rows, subject levels, etc.)",     y = "median fit time (ms, log scale)",     colour = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "top",         panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) mixeff_rows <- subset(benchmark, engine == "mixeff")  ggplot(mixeff_rows,        aes(scale_value, speedup_vs_lme4,            colour = scenario_label, group = scenario_label)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 2, colour = "grey50") +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   scale_x_log10() +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 8, by = 1)) +   labs(     x = "design scale",     y = "speedup over lme4 (×)",     colour = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom",         legend.direction = "vertical",         panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +   guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 1))

what: What does inference method choice cost?
title: Benchmarking mixeff
text: Speed one part inference decision. route table also needs calibration evidence: often method rejects null, often detects small alternative, whether interval routes cover small alternative. lightweight simulation scaffold records three quantities four fixtures: interior fit, boundary-like fit, reduced-rank fit, small-group fit. checked-summary produced default fast mode, excludes bootstrap routes script runs seconds. Slow mode adds bootstrap bootstrap-LRT routes local evidence gathering. plot shows whichever metrics available route. P-value routes contribute Type error power; interval routes contribute coverage. Routes wired current package version remain CSV n_reps = 0, downstream reports can distinguish “run fast mode” “part contract”.

title: Demystifying Random-Effects Formulas
text: random intercept simplest random-effects structure: subjects may differ average score, fixed time effect across subjects. often right choice, always reasonable starting point. explain_model() spells model assumes reports random slopes added. random-effects card two header lines worth pausing . wrote: term typed . canonical: parser hands downstream — optimizer, audit, inference contract actually see. identical (1 | subject) already canonical form, identity positive signal: nothing rewritten. two diverge elaborate formulas. (1 | clinic/site) rewritten (1 | clinic) + (1 | clinic:site); implicit intercepts made explicit; double-bar shorthand expands split-block form. canonical: line read model engine actually fit. remaining lines decode term . named form: restates function call names grouping factor, intercept, slopes (), covariance family. scope: plain-language sentence. covariance support report parameter cost design sufficiency. Design notes: section bottom records facts data fitted formula use. time varies within subject, subject-level time slope structurally possible. reported note, correction: whether add (time | subject) scientific question whether subjects plausibly differ time slopes, mechanical one package can .
code: intercept_only <- compile_model(score ~ time + (1 | subject), study) explain_model(intercept_only) #> Random effects explanation: #>   formula: score ~ 1 + time + (1 | subject) #>  #> Random effects: #>   r0: #>     wrote:      (1 | subject) #>     canonical:  (1 | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = TRUE, slopes = NULL, cov = "scalar") #>     scope:      `subject` units may differ in average outcome. #>     covariance: scalar; theta parameters: 1 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  intercept=not_assessed #>  #> Design notes: #>   scope_note: `time` varies within `subject`, so a `subject`-level slope is structurally possible

what: What changes when you add a random slope?
title: Demystifying Random-Effects Formulas
text: (1 + time | subject) requests two-dimensional random effect subject: one baseline coefficient one time coefficient, fitted covariance . fitting, parameterization() shows model’s internal coordinates. brief detour explains . random-effects covariance matrix Σ — 2×2 matrix describing variance intercept, variance time slope, covariance — optimized directly. Instead, engine searches vector θ maps lower-triangular factor Λ via Σ = σ² Λ Λᵀ. reasons practical: θ-space minimum number free parameters, better-conditioned searching Σ directly, implied Σ positive-semidefinite construction. parameterization Bates et al. (2015, JSS 67(1)) describe lme4; MixedModels.jl engine behind mixeff inherit . 2×2 random-effects matrix, Λ three free entries — three rows see . theta_value optimizer worked ; lambda_value places number back matrix. match mapping identity structure; mismatch point parameterization bug rather modelling fact. practical use table boundary-fit detection: θ entry pinned zero, corresponding variance covariance reached edge parameter space, random-effects covariance reduced-rank. headliner vignette walks case end--end.
code: full <- compile_model(score ~ time + (1 + time | subject), study) explain_model(full) #> Random effects explanation: #>   formula: score ~ 1 + time + (1 + time | subject) #>  #> Random effects: #>   r0: #>     wrote:      (1 + time | subject) #>     canonical:  (1 + time | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = TRUE, slopes = time, cov = "full") #>     scope:      `subject` units differ in baseline and `time` slope; the model estimates whether these are associated. #>     covariance: full; theta parameters: 3 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  intercept=not_assessed; time=present full_fit <- lmm(   score ~ time + (1 + time | subject),   study,   control = mm_control(verbose = -1) ) parameterization(full_fit)$table[, c("theta_name", "theta_value", "lambda_value")] #>                     theta_name theta_value lambda_value #> 1 theta[0:intercept,intercept]   1.0706644    1.0706644 #> 2      theta[0:time,intercept]   0.1222131    0.1222131 #> 3           theta[0:time,time]   0.5466997    0.5466997

what: What do split blocks and || mean?
title: Demystifying Random-Effects Formulas
text: formula (1 | subject) + (0 + time | subject) uses two separate blocks grouping factor. fixes intercept-slope covariance zero. (1 + time || subject) shorthand split-block model. One caveat factor appears inside ||: mixeff decorrelates everything block, including factor’s level contrasts — treatment-coded contrast receives independent variance within-factor covariances estimated (announced via covariance_assumption diagnostic reason double_bar_factor_term). Implementations differ — lme4’s || leaves factor terms intact full within-factor covariance block — matching external fit, write intended expansion explicitly: (1 | g) + (0 + f | g) + (0 + x | g) keeps f’s level covariances, (1 + f + x || g) .
code: split_blocks <- compile_model(   score ~ time + (1 | subject) + (0 + time | subject),   study ) explain_model(split_blocks) #> Random effects explanation: #>   formula: score ~ 1 + time + (1 | subject) + (0 + time | subject) #>  #> Random effects: #>   subject has 2 separate random-effect blocks. #>   r0: #>     wrote:      (1 | subject) #>     canonical:  (1 | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = TRUE, slopes = NULL, cov = "scalar") #>     scope:      `subject` units may differ in average outcome. #>     covariance: scalar; theta parameters: 1 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  intercept=not_assessed #>   r1: #>     wrote:      (0 + time | subject) #>     canonical:  (0 + time | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = FALSE, slopes = time, cov = "scalar") #>     scope:      `subject` units may differ in their `time` slope. #>     covariance: scalar; theta parameters: 1 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  time=present #>  #> Relationship between blocks: #>   r0 <-> r1 (Intercept <-> time): separate random-effect blocks fix the covariance between `Intercept` and `time` to zero. #>  #> Design notes: #>   random_slope_without_intercept: random slope term omits a random intercept; this leaves baseline grouping dependence unmodeled unless represented elsewhere #>   covariance_assumption: the covariance between 'Intercept' and 'time' is fixed at zero by separate random-effect blocks double_bar <- compile_model(score ~ time + (1 + time || subject), study) explain_model(double_bar) #> Random effects explanation: #>   formula: score ~ 1 + time + (1 + time || subject) #>  #> Random effects: #>   subject has 2 separate random-effect blocks. #>   r0: #>     wrote:      (1 + time || subject) #>     canonical:  (1 | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = TRUE, slopes = NULL, cov = "scalar") #>     scope:      `subject` units may differ in average outcome. #>     covariance: scalar; theta parameters: 1 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  intercept=not_assessed #>   r1: #>     wrote:      (1 + time || subject) #>     canonical:  (0 + time | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = FALSE, slopes = time, cov = "scalar") #>     scope:      `subject` units may differ in their `time` slope. #>     covariance: scalar; theta parameters: 1 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  time=present #>  #> Relationship between blocks: #>   r0 <-> r1 (Intercept <-> time): double-bar syntax fixes the covariance between `Intercept` and `time` to zero. #>  #> Design notes: #>   covariance_assumption: the covariance between 'Intercept' and 'time' is fixed at zero by || syntax

what: What are the three kinds of help?
title: Demystifying Random-Effects Formulas
text: audit surface separates three situations often mixed together ordinary model output. First, structural impossibility: requested random slope estimated variable vary within group. dose constant within subject. Second, low information budget: requested full covariance can estimable principle still little grouping-level support. package reports parameter count observed levels. Third, unmodeled--possible: fixed effect varies within group, random-effects formula include corresponding slope. quiet design note saw (1 | subject).
code: impossible <- compile_model(score ~ dose + (1 + dose | subject), between_study) explain_model(impossible) #> Random effects explanation: #>   formula: score ~ 1 + dose + (1 + dose | subject) #>  #> Random effects: #>   r0: #>     wrote:      (1 + dose | subject) #>     canonical:  (1 + dose | subject) #>     named form: re(group = subject, intercept = TRUE, slopes = dose, cov = "full") #>     scope:      `subject` units differ in baseline and `dose` slope; the model estimates whether these are associated. #>     covariance: full; theta parameters: 3 #>     support:    sufficient; group levels: 30; min rows/group: 5; median rows/group: 5 #>     variation:  dose=absent; intercept=not_assessed #>  #> Possible repairs, not applied automatically: #>   1. structural_refusal: `dose` does not vary within `subject`, so a `subject`-level `dose` slope cannot be estimated from this design. low_info <- compile_model(score ~ time + (1 + time | subject), low_study) diagnostics(low_info)$table[, c("code", "severity", "message")] #>                  code severity #> 1 covariance_too_rich  warning #> 2 covariance_too_rich  warning #>                                                                              message #> 1 4 levels are below the v0 full-covariance threshold 15 for 3 covariance parameters #> 2 4 levels are below the v0 full-covariance threshold 15 for 3 covariance parameters diagnostics(intercept_only)$table[, c("code", "severity", "message")] #>         code severity #> 1 scope_note     info #> 2 scope_note     info #>                                                                               message #> 1 `time` varies within `subject`, so a `subject`-level slope is structurally possible #> 2 `time` varies within `subject`, so a `subject`-level slope is structurally possible

the model estimates whether these are associated. opts <- random_options(intercept_only, group = subject, slope = time) opts$options[, c("formula", "theta_parameters", "design_status", "current")] #>                                formula theta_parameters design_status current #> 1                        (1 | subject)                1    sufficient    TRUE #> 2                 (0 + time | subject)                1    sufficient   FALSE #> 3 (1 | subject) + (0 + time | subject)                2    sufficient   FALSE #> 4                (1 + time || subject)                2    sufficient   FALSE #> 5                 (1 + time | subject)                3    sufficient   FALSE

what: Why is there no recommendation row?
title: Demystifying Random-Effects Formulas
text: mixeff treats formula explanations audit problem, model-selection problem. package reports current model, nearby spellings, assumptions, data-support facts. Choosing among scientifically different random-effects structures remains part analysis design. compare_covariance() gives another view principle: full, diagonal, scalar covariance families displayed side side, without preferred row.

what: Four things that will bite, and the fix
title: Migrating from lme4
text: 1. Coefficient names match lme4 exactly. Since 0.2.0, fixef(), summary() tables, vcov() dimnames, mm_lincomb() weight names use lme4’s naming column order ("recipeB", "temperature.L", "recipeB:temperature.L"), name-keyed lme4 code drop-compatible. (Earlier versions used engine encoding like "recipe: B"; wrote normalisation shims , delete .) 2. Grouped binomial responses. glmm() accepts cbind(successes, failures) spelling like glmer: 3. default GLMM estimator glmer’s. glmm() defaults fast profiled (PIRLS) estimator whose coefficients match glmer() exactly; prints notice saying . glmer-equivalent (joint Laplace) estimates, ask : 4. || factor means full decorrelation. mixeff, || fixes every covariance block zero — including covariances among factor’s level contrasts (treatment-coded contrast gets independent variance). lme4’s || split factor terms: factor keeps full within-factor covariance block. (1 + cond + x || subj) factor cond fits strictly larger model lme4 mixeff, two disagree parameter count (hence df, AIC, — fitted within-factor covariance non-zero — optimum ). mixeff announces situation compile time info diagnostic (covariance_assumption, reason double_bar_factor_term). reproduce lme4’s model family exactly, write expansion explicitly give factor correlated block:
code: glmm(cbind(incidence, size - incidence) ~ period + (1 | herd),      lme4::cbpp, family = binomial()) glmm(y ~ x + (1 | g), data, family = binomial(),      method = "joint_laplace") # mixeff `||`: independent variances for every column, factor levels included glmm(y ~ cond * x + (1 + cond + x || subj), data, family = binomial())  # lme4-equivalent family: the factor keeps its within-factor covariance block glmm(y ~ cond * x + (1 | subj) + (0 + cond | subj) + (0 + x | subj),      data, family = binomial())

title: Why mixeff?
text: small repeated-measures study: 18 subjects, 10 daily reaction-time measurements , intercepts slopes nearly perfectly correlated construction. kind design produces singular fit modern engine.

what: C. Expose when asymptotics are weak, then offer bootstrap
title: Why mixeff?
text: mixeff two jobs inference side. First, tells asymptotic methods (Wald z, Satterthwaite, Kenward-Roger) unreliable fit. , bootstrap defensible route, offers labelled, first-class peer asymptotic methods — fallback trick. inference_options() audit verb judgment. enumerates inference methods available current fit, gives one closed-enum reason status, names verb call invoke . Two routes available fit: asymptotic Wald z (immediate, labelled low reliability), bootstrap (~seconds, labelled replicate count Monte-Carlo SE). Satterthwaite Kenward-Roger refuse stable reason — *_unavailable_at_boundary — boundary fit variance-parameter derivative need defined. fact math, bug. asymptotic Wald row carries warrant. summary() now prints reliability_reason next reliability: degrees_of_freedom_unavailable_so_z_substituted closed-enum warrant: t reference distribution requested target df computed boundary fit, standard normal substituted. number real; grade calibrated. defensible p-value fit, route contrast() method = "bootstrap". Rust engine simulates constrained null, refits replicate, returns labelled inference row plus run payload (boundary rate, MCSE, replicate count) audit. bootstrap p-value available, method named, run payload makes simulation provenance explicit. boundary rate visible singular fits propagate boundary behaviour bootstrap replicates — honest, bug. mcse quantifies Monte-Carlo uncertainty p-value estimate; raise nsim tighter MCSE. three states reported quantity: available named method closed-enum warrant, unavailable stable reason code, typed error. fourth state package guesses.
code: opt <- inference_options(fit, "days", nsim = 200) opt$table[, c("method", "expected_status",               "expected_reliability_reason", "current")] #>              method expected_status #> 1 asymptotic_wald_z       available #> 2     satterthwaite    not_assessed #> 3     kenward_roger    not_assessed #> 4         bootstrap       available #> 5     bootstrap_lrt    not_assessed #> 6 cluster_bootstrap    not_assessed #> 7        profile_ci    not_assessed #>                      expected_reliability_reason current #> 1                     asymptotic_wald_z_fallback    TRUE #> 2          satterthwaite_unavailable_at_boundary   FALSE #> 3          kenward_roger_unavailable_at_boundary   FALSE #> 4               bootstrap_monte_carlo_replicates   FALSE #> 5                      bootstrap_lrt_requires_ml   FALSE #> 6 bootstrap_cluster_resample_p_value_unavailable   FALSE #> 7             profile_ci_unavailable_at_boundary   FALSE inf <- inference_table(fit)$table inf[, c("term", "method", "status", "reliability", "reliability_reason")] #>          term            method    status reliability #> 1 (Intercept) asymptotic_wald_z available         low #> 2        days asymptotic_wald_z available         low #>           reliability_reason #> 1 asymptotic_wald_z_fallback #> 2 asymptotic_wald_z_fallback ct <- contrast(fit, c(0, 1), method = "bootstrap",                bootstrap = bootstrap_control(nsim = 200, seed = 1)) ct$table[, c("contrast", "estimate", "p_value",              "method", "status", "reliability")] #>   contrast estimate     p_value    method    status reliability #> 1       c1 9.250949 0.004975124 bootstrap available         low  run <- ct$table$details[[1]]$bootstrap data.frame(   successful_replicates = run$successful_replicates,   boundary_rate         = round(run$boundary_rate, 3),   mcse                  = round(run$mcse, 4) ) #>   successful_replicates boundary_rate  mcse #> 1                   200          0.46 0.005

Check line first. converged_interior good outcome: optimizer found clean solution every variance component comfortably positive, can read rest summary face value. status changes something needs attention — example, variance component collapses zero (data show detectable variation term), status reports boundary fit, affected row flagged variance-components table, p-values switch conservative, clearly labeled method. short: healthy fit says converged_interior, unhealthy one tells went wrong instead leaving notice. Variance components. Subject (Intercept) -subject spread baseline reaction time (SD ≈ 25 ms); Subject Days -subject spread sensitivity sleep loss (SD ≈ 6 ms per day); correlation association (+0.07 — essentially none). residual standard deviation within-subject noise left . Fixed effects. Estimate population-level coefficient. Days = 10.47 means , average, reaction time increases 10.5 ms per day sleep deprivation. method column names p-value computed — satterthwaite, finite-sample t test whose df column (≈ 17) scale 18 subjects, 180 raw rows. mixeff never reports number without naming method behind . fits Satterthwaite degrees freedom computed (example variance component boundary), summary shows clearly labeled asymptotic Wald z rows instead; method column always tells one got. Inference status. One row per coefficient stating much trust test: status says whether computed, reliability grades , reliability_reason names engine’s warrant grade — satterthwaite_finite_difference_approximation, meaning degrees freedom come finite-difference approximation, standard route method (graded moderate). engine notes print underneath. text: formula two-sided lme4-style formula. data data.frame. family supported GLMM family object family constructor. certified 1.0 surface : binomial() "logit", "probit", "cloglog" links; poisson() "log" "sqrt" links; Gamma() "log" link; negative binomial (NB2, "log" link) via mm_negative_binomial() (theta estimated, like lme4::glmer.nb()) MASS::negative.binomial(theta) (fixed theta). random Reserved native random-effect constructor path. weights Optional prior weights. binomial models trial counts proportion responses; weights must positive finite. offset Optional fixed linear-predictor offset; values must finite. subset, na.action, contrasts Reserved future parity lmm(). method GLMM estimation method. "pirls_profiled" default fast-PIRLS profiled path. "joint_laplace" requests labelled joint Laplace route requires nAGQ <= 1. joint route tracks lme4 joint-Laplace reference far closely profiled path high-baseline models, higher optimizer cost; cap cost mm_control(max_feval = ). default profiled path glmer's estimator coefficients match glmer() exactly; method left default, glmm() emits informational notice effect (suppress mm_control(verbose = -1)). Use method = "joint_laplace" glmer-equivalent estimates. nAGQ Number adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature points. 1 Laplace setting. Values 1 allowed profiled path rejected method = "joint_laplace" R wrapper. inference Requested inference method. control list mm_control(). ... Reserved future use.

title: Control mixeff fitting behavior — mm_control
text: verbose Integer verbosity level. Use -1 suppress automatic model explanation (GLMM estimator notice). max_feval Optional positive integer capping optimizer's objective evaluations. useful glmm() method = "joint_laplace", whose native joint optimizer otherwise runs engine-chosen budget. NULL (default) leaves engine default place. optimizer Optional optimizer name, overriding driver's automatic choice. One "auto" (default behaviour), "bobyqa", "newuoa", "cobyla", "pattern_search", "trust_bq", PRIMA variants ("prima_bobyqa", "prima_cobyla", "prima_lincoa", "prima_newuoa"). unsupported -compiled choice raises typed error rather silently falling back. NULL/"auto" keep automatic selection. start Optional numeric warm-start vector covariance parameters (theta). length must match model's theta dimension (engine validates ). NULL (default) cold-starts. ftol_rel, ftol_abs Optional positive relative/absolute convergence tolerances objective. NULL keeps engine default. xtol_rel Optional positive relative convergence tolerance optimizer parameters. NULL keeps engine default.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: audit() now single audit verb, dispatching compiled specs fits; audit_design() forwards deprecation warning removed later. df_for_contrast() reporting_table() now return mm_* objects $table (plus $df former, $sections reporting_table(section = "")), matching every sibling analysis verb, instead bare classed vector / data frame. spec-accepting inspection verbs (changes(), parameterization(), reproducibility(), random_blocks(), optimizer_certificate(), reporting_table(), audit()) name first argument object (previously fit, misleading specs). Fit-inference verbs keep fit. model.frame.mm_lmm() keeps formula — name imposed stats::model.frame() generic. confint() presents "asymptotic" canonical method name (package-wide term closed-form Wald interval); "wald" remains accepted synonym. Computation unchanged. drop1() now matches stats::drop1() marginality semantics: default, main effects participating interaction offered dropping. explicit non-marginal scope still honoured fits normally. result gains status reason columns.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: Fixed-effect coefficient names now match lme4/model.matrix() exactly — "recipeB", "temperature.L", "recipeB:temperature.L" — model.matrix() column order, every programmatic surface: fixef(), coef(), summary() tables, vcov() dimnames, confint(), contrast() mm_lincomb() weight names, tidy(), emmeans, predict(). Previously mixeff used engine encoding ("recipe: B") different interaction column order, linear combinations coefficient lookups copy-pasted lme4 code silently misaligned. Code written old names must switch lme4 forms. engine encoding still appears inside engine-rendered explain()/audit() prose. ranef() column names stripped lme4 form ("modalityAudio"); VarCorr() printing unchanged. Fits saved saveRDS() older versions lack stored name map re-fit.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: Engine pin bumped 4a2abb3: hardens convergence runtime contracts. Non-marginal designs (y ~ b + :b) now fit match lme4 exactly (previously refused). Convergence labelling conservative — fit reaches flat/boundary region without certified stationary point now reported not_optimized (previously sometimes converged_reduced_rank) small maximal random-slope models; genuine reduced-rank optima (e.g. lme4::Dyestuff2) still report converged_reduced_rank. prior pin, ee0c717, carried audit-render wording batch (policy recommendations phrased options, boundary-sentence deduplication, humanized summary-view jargon). Earlier cycle pin moved 3b6ec69 (one commit past v1.0.0-rc.1), fixes native crossed-LMM trust-region start. Crossed-design fits route trust-region optimizer may land slightly different (better-started) optima. bundled mixeff-rs engine now pinned first tagged release, v1.0.0-rc.1 (3332f3e). two response-batch diagnostic reasons new release (sink_stopped, adaptive_refinement) registered R-side reason registry, coverage contract (every engine reason R-side entry) holds.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: VarCorr() correlations now stored full precision numeric columns $table (correlation, plus correlation2, … groups three random terms; NA pair exists). Previously column 2-decimal display string, forcing callers parse text costing ~2% precision well-determined correlations. Printing unchanged: rounding 2 decimals now happens display time.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: glmm() now fits NB2 negative-binomial models (log link) default profiled path. family = mm_negative_binomial() estimates size parameter theta alongside model (lme4::glmer.nb() route); family = MASS::negative.binomial(theta) mm_negative_binomial(theta) fits conditional fixed theta. fitted/fixed theta recorded fit$family$nb_theta. method = "joint_laplace" yet wired family pinned engine refused typed error.

what: Clearer user-facing text (UX parity pass vs lme4)
title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: 13-scenario side--side battery lme4 (graded independently) drove cleanup every surface engine internals leaked user-facing text: GLMM summaries withheld inference now explain plainly fast default certify SEs/z/p re-fit method = "joint_laplace"; engine’s covariance-geometry warrant moved behind print(summary(fit), verbose = TRUE). Unsupported-family errors list supported families point lme4::glmer() rest. new-grouping-level prediction error describes R-level remedies (re.form = NA, allow.new.levels = TRUE) instead Rust API names, bridge errors longer print duplicated “Caused ” chain. anova() prints compact lme4-shaped table; single-df terms display equivalent F statistic (matching lmerTest), provenance/list columns stay $table. Aliased (rank-deficient) coefficients display NA explicit note, instead misleading 0. print() longer emits artifact/crate provenance line (available fit$schema); confint()’s internal certification label translated display.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: parameterization() fitted GLMM reported compile-time Lambda template (1s 0s) theta_value instead fitted theta; now splices fitted values index (LMM fits unaffected; pre-fit specs keep honest template). Logical random slopes now carry lme4-style ranef() column names ("xTRUE"), consistent fixed-effect naming conditional variance arrays. glmm(method = "joint_laplace") emits -front runtime notice: joint route optimizes engine-chosen budget inside single silent native call can take minutes large data (cap mm_control(max_feval = )). Summary notes completed joint fits longer imply unusable fit engine’s convergence label not_assessed/not_optimized (label reliability tracked upstream); point verify_convergence(). Bootstrap-based inference 200+ replicates announces scale single silent native call. lmm()/glmm() now emit rescaling advisory continuous predictor scale far 1 (matching lme4’s “predictors different scales” guidance): fits can converge poorly, scale() cheap fix. notice, refusal; suppress mm_control(verbose = -1). lmm()/glmm() document optimization silent non-interruptible within one native call, bounded budgets.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: New profile.mm_lmm() method: returns mm_profile object engine’s certified profile-likelihood payload ($table one row per parameter; REML fixed effects carry explicit profile_beta_unavailable_under_reml reason instead dropped). confint() profile reproduces confint(fit, method = "profile"). lmm() now refuses multivariate cbind(y1, y2) responses plain error (fit outcome separately); shared-theta multivariate models deferred post-release. glmm() continues accept cbind(successes, failures) binomial responses.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: Population-level predictions (re.form = NA ~0) longer require random-effect grouping columns newdata, matching predict(lmer/glmer, re.form = NA). fixed-part variables needed; conditional predictions (re.form = NULL) still require full formula’s variables.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: Ordered factors now coded orthonormal polynomial contrasts (contr.poly) fit time, matching R/lme4 defaults, instead treatment coding. Fixed effects, random-slope (Z) coding, logLik/AIC/BIC, predictions now reach parity lme4 ordered-factor models (e.g. lme4::cake). Coefficient names still use mixeff’s engine encoding (temperature: .L) pending lme4-identical renaming layer. global ordered-contrast option contr.poly, ordered column carries explicit non-poly contrasts attribute, lmm()/glmm() refuse typed mm_arg_error rather silently diverge requested coding. Behavior change: one-level ordered factor now errors loudly (polynomial contrasts require least two levels) instead silently degenerating empty/near-empty design.

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: New verb verify_convergence(): re-runs fitted LMM engine’s bounded verification workflow (restart optimum, jittered restarts, opt-alternate-optimizer consensus) reports engine’s verdict per-run objective/theta/beta deltas. check audit surface already pointed uncertain optima; verdict wording engine-owned. consensus defaults FALSE vendored build compiles without optional nlopt backend, whose absence consensus pass otherwise report spurious fragile. changes() now prints one plain-language sentence per recorded change (e.g. Fitted covariance (1 | s): requested rank 1, fitted rank 0 [reduced_rank].) instead dumping raw stage table. certificate-time rank statement treated canonical record boundary event, design/covariance restatements repeated (remain $table). fit whose optimizer stopped early now says explicitly (none: structural change made; optimizer stopped early (fit status \not_optimized`).) instead showing misleadingunchanged / formula display` row. automatic pre-fit explain_model() block emitted lmm()/glmm() now travels message stream (typed mm_explanation_notice condition) instead stdout, suppressMessages() knitr’s message = FALSE can quiet . remains default; mm_control(verbose = -1) still suppresses entirely, explicit print(explain_model(spec)) still writes stdout. summary() GLMM now defaults tests = "coefficients" (matching lme4::glmer). fit method certify fixed-effect inference (default pirls_profiled estimator), SE/z/p columns still withheld — Notes: line now states , engine-certified Wald inference available method = "joint_laplace" fit. Previously default summary() printed NA columns explanation. summary() fit whose optimizer stopped without certifying optimum (e.g. fit status not_optimized) now repeats state plain-language Notes: line directly coefficient tests, instead relying header status line alone. Displayed p-values now render format.pval(): underflowed p-value prints < 1e-16 instead 0.000000e+00. Stored values unchanged. singular-fit print() footer advertises random_options(spec, group = ...) call can actually run fit (slope candidate exists); previously printed hint error fit printed .

title: mixeff 0.2.0
text: Grouping-variable coercion: lmm() / glmm() now coerce non-categorical grouping variable (e.g. integer subject id numeric item code) factor random-effects structure, matching lme4/nlme/glmmTMB. Previously column rejected native fit “grouping factor categorical”. coercion announced via suppressible notice (class mm_grouping_coercion_notice; silence mm_control(verbose = -1)), never silent. Surfaced --wild OSF glmer reproduction crossed (1 | ID) + (1 | Title) effects. Certified GLMM Wald inference: fit method = "joint_laplace", summary(), confint(method = "wald"), contrast(), tidy() now report engine-certified fixed-effect standard errors, Wald z statistics, p-values match lme4::glmer() within tolerance. default method = "pirls_profiled" path certified fixed-effect inference, four surfaces withhold SE/z/p (returning NA reason vcov_status "unsupported") rather fabricate uncertified working Hessian — consistent package’s “fake certainty” contract. Conditional prediction standard errors intervals: predict() mm_lmm / mm_glmm re.form = NULL now routes se.fit interval engine’s prediction-variance payload, includes random-effect (BLUP) variance fixed/random covariance — surface lme4::predict.merMod offer . LMMs get conditional se.fit plus "confidence" "prediction" intervals; GLMMs get conditional se.fit "confidence" intervals link response scale (variance propagated link engine). engine certifies rows method = "joint_laplace" fits — via post-fit profiled-optimum certificate — default pirls_profiled fits, default estimator now reports conditional SEs . Rows engine certify withheld, fabricated: uncertified fits (e.g. singular fits, whose certificate never issued) unseen grouping levels allow.new.levels = TRUE return NA engine’s reason mm_reason attribute. Population (re.form = NA) SEs/intervals unchanged. GLMM prediction (future-observation) intervals: predict(interval = "prediction") now works conditional, response-scale GLMM predictions. Bounds quantiles plug-predictive distribution (family conditional distribution mixed link-scale fitted-mean uncertainty via Gauss–Hermite quadrature), integers count families support points Bernoulli; interval least wide corresponding confidence interval. Typed refusals remain link-scale requests (future observations response-scale objects), population-level requests, grouped binomial fits (future trial count representable newdata). || factor-term semantics documented contract-tested: mixeff, zero-correlation syntax fully decorrelates block — factor’s treatment-coded level contrasts get independent variances within-factor covariances (principled reading, shared afex::mixed(expand_re = TRUE), glmmTMB::diag(), MixedModels.jl zerocorr()). lme4’s || instead leaves factor terms intact full within-factor covariance block, formula fits larger (-parameterized) model . Fits announce situation info diagnostic (covariance_assumption, reason double_bar_factor_term) naming correlated-block rewrite ((0 + f | g)); lme4-migration formula vignettes carry recipe. Binomial response coercion: glmm() family = binomial() now accepts logical response (coerced 0/1 silently) two-level factor response (coerced second level success, announced via suppressible mm_factor_coercion message), matching stats::glm() / lme4::glmer(). factor number levels aborts typed mm_data_error. Previously responses surfaced opaque engine error. update() mm_lmm / mm_glmm: formula edits (. ~ . - x, preserving random-effect bars ||), REML/weights/family/ offset/method/control overrides, new data, evaluate = FALSE. broom / broom.mixed support: tidy(), glance(), augment() methods mm_lmm / mm_glmm (registered generics). confint.mm_glmm(): asymptotic Wald intervals GLMM fixed effects (refuses profile/bootstrap typed reason). predict.mm_glmm(): type = "link"/"response", population conditional (re.form = NULL/NA) predictions allow.new.levels, replacing previous refusal. Validated engine’s fitted(). GLMM fixed-effect inference: contrast.mm_glmm() (Wald), drop1.mm_glmm() (refit LRT), anova.mm_glmm() (sequential LRT nested models). GLMM estimator transparency: native method = "joint_laplace" path certified lme4::glmer within tolerance, glmm() now emits informational notice (class mm_estimator_notice) default pirls_profiled estimator used, since coefficients glmer-equivalent (use method = "joint_laplace" parity).

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: First public release mixeff, audit-first R wrapper around mixedmodels Rust crate. package distributed via R-Universe bbuchsbaum.r-universe.dev; upstream nlopt feature-gate PR lands CRAN distribution tracked separately ships 0.2.0.

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: rextendr/extendr_api bridge vendored upstream mixedmodels crate; CRAN-compatible build cargo vendor + vendor.tar.xz reconstitution R CMD INSTALL time. mm_parse_formula() — R/Rust formula round-trip primitive. mm_formula_manifest() — capability discovery bridge. mm_json_negotiate() mm_json_known_schemas() — schema versioning gate; mismatched artifacts raise mm_schema_error rather silently misparse. Interrupt FFI: Ctrl-C long Rust fit cleanly returns R. Typed condition catalog (mm_condition base class): mm_formula_error, mm_data_error, mm_schema_error, mm_design_refusal, mm_inference_unavailable, mm_fit_error, mm_not_identifiable, mm_fit_not_optimized.

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: compile_model() — formula + data → semantic IR + design audit, fitting. audit_design() — structured design audit; raises mm_design_refusal non-identifiable terms optimization runs. explain_model() — auto-printed lmm() / glmm() fit. Translates random term named-argument form, prints per-block English gloss (authored Rust), emits mandatory random slopes added. sentinel intercept-random terms. random_options() — opt-map nearby random-effect spellings grouping factor (punt, slope-, split-uncorrelated, double-bar, full). “recommended” column; preference ordering. compare_covariance() — full / diagonal / scalar comparison per random term. changes(), diagnostics(), fit_status(), parameterization(), roles(), as_json(), is_singular(). lmm() — REML/ML linear mixed-model fit via upstream Rust LinearMixedModel engine. Returns serializable mm_lmm carrying JSON artifact, parsed state, beta/theta/sigma/logLik/deviance, fitted values, residuals, random effects, varcorr. lme4-style extractor surface mm_lmm: fixef, ranef, coef, VarCorr, sigma, logLik, deviance, AIC, BIC, nobs, formula, model.frame, df.residual, fitted, residuals(type="response"), basic predict(). Pedagogical DiagnosticCode variants surfaced Rust: ScopeNote, SupportNote, SyntaxExpansion, CovarianceAssumption, StructuralRefusal. Random-term card schema (RandomTermCard) shipped per random term term_id, original_fragment, canonical_fragment, group, blocks, implied_constraints, design_support. R9 “advice creep” contract enforced test--advice.R: strings "suggested starting model", "recommend", "", "try ... instead", "drop random slope", "model, different font" appear package output. Vignettes: intro.Rmd, lmm-basics.Rmd, demystifying-formulas.Rmd.

what: Phase 2 — saveRDS round-trip and lazy extractors
title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: saveRDS / readRDS survives without live Rust handle — artifact source truth. revive() — rebuilds Rust handle durable artifact live cache needed. fit_handle_alive(), getME(fit, name) X, Z, theta, Lambda, cnms, flist, Gp, lower, devcomp, optinfo. model.matrix(type=), vcov(type="fixed"). random_blocks() — per-block decomposition random-effects matrix. optimizer_certificate() — convergence status, iterations, objective trace, verification trace. inference_table() — per-coefficient method/status/reliability rows read Rust inference contract. reproducibility() — Rust-authored reproducibility envelope (engine version, schema version, seed, optimizer fingerprint). is_singular() — boolean predicate optimizer certificate. Vignette: saving--reviving.Rmd.

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: contrast(fit, L, rhs, method) — fixed-effect contrast front door. Methods: "auto", "satterthwaite", "kenward_roger", "bootstrap", "asymptotic", "none". Returns method / status / reliability / reason columns; never fabricates p-values engine certify method. test_effect(fit, term, method) — term-level hypothesis tests. Bootstrap bootstrap-LRT methods backed upstream Rust bootstrap entry points; cluster bootstrap recognized documented estimator-distribution (certified p-value schema 1.0.0). inference_table(fit, method) — multi-row inference table. df_for_contrast(), estimability() — placeholders return NA stable reason 0.2.0 wires Rust certificates end--end. anova() — single multi-model. drop1.mm_lmm(). confint(method = "wald") — Wald asymptotic interval flagged status "not_certified_by_rust_inference_contract". confint(method = "bootstrap") — full-model bootstrap intervals percentile / basic selection bootstrap metadata. bootstrap_control() — control object bootstrap-backed methods (replicate count, seed, failed-refit policy). Vignettes: inference.Rmd, inference--lme4-says-.Rmd.

what: Phase 4 — GLMM boundary and LMM lifecycle
title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: glmm() — Phase 4 boundary. upstream Rust bridge yet expose GLMM fit primitive, glmm() validates family/link request, compiles model spec, raises typed mm_fit_error expected family / link / nAGQ metadata bridge primitive lands. (Real GLMM fitting queued 0.2.0; upstream FFI available.) simulate.mm_lmm() — simulate fitted LMM using durable artifact state. refit() — refit new response. compare() — model comparison auditable validity status. Multi-model anova() drop1() mm_lmm objects. parametric_bootstrap() — parametric bootstrap distribution fixed-effect tests. Manifest capabilities simulate/inference exposed via bridge contract. Vignettes: glmm.Rmd (boundary walkthrough), benchmarking.Rmd, reporting-lmms.Rmd.

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: mm_control() — flat named list mirroring lmerControl. Honored fields include optimizer, optimizer_max_iter, optimizer_xtol_abs, optimizer_ftol_abs, reml, nAGQ, verify_convergence, parallel_threads, seed, verbose, thresholds, schema_version, bridge_timeout_s. mm_thresholds() — design/identifiability thresholds (byte-equivalent upstream compiler_contract_v0_prd.md §8). Parity ledger (inst/extdata/expected-mismatches.json) — every divergence lme4 classified (expected_mismatch / upstream_bug / unsupported) bounds enforced tests/testthat/helper-parity-scoreboard.R. Parity scoreboard (test-parity-scoreboard.R) — emits structured artifact recording observed differences tolerances classic lme4 parity baseline. Speedup vs lme4 included scaling benchmark (benchmarks/lme4-scaling/) ranges ~2× (small balanced LMMs) ~5× (correlated random slopes ≥30 grouping levels).

title: mixeff 0.1.0
text: mixeff drop-lme4 replacement. bit-exact numerical reproduction lme4. model-selection random-effects recommendation engine (recommend_model(), auto_random_effects(), fix_singularity(), make_it_converge()). lme4::lmer / lme4::glmer masked attach.
